outcome_text: The specific tasks and methods used to implement the tasks that the AI system will support are defined (e.g., classifiers, generative models, recommenders).
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system task specification and method selection, including defining the specific tasks an AI system will perform, selecting appropriate implementation methods (classification, generation, recommendation), and documenting the relationship between tasks and implementation approaches.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system task specification and method selection, including defining the specific tasks an AI system will perform, selecting appropriate implementation methods (classification, generation, recommendation), and documenting the relationship between tasks and implementation approaches.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-2.1 (MAP) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.570, cosine: 0.639). Secondary: RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.629), RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.616).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.7/10 (raw 0.570). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge, methods]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [approaches, appropriate, classification, defining, documenting, generation] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, explainability, fairness, human, impact, methodologies] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.872 (P=0.865, R=0.880). Balanced P/R — the 35-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.639 > STS 0.570 (gap +0.069). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, knowledge, methods] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, methods]. NIST AI RMF-only: [approaches, appropriate, classification, defining, documenting, generation]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, explainability, fairness, human, impact, methodologies].
